topic: Persistence and health issues of PFAS
postText: PFAS has been linked to numerous health issues and is one of the most persistent problems in the environment. The materials deployed at Selfridge Air National Guard Base could stop chemicals from further contaminating groundwater.

claimText: PFAS are a large group of man-made chemicals known for their resistance to heat, water, and stains, making them useful in countless products, but they also pose health risks like cancers, liver damage, and immune system issues.
topic: Properties and health risks of PFAS

claimText: Firefighters have relied on firefighting foams for decades, but many contain PFAS, or “forever chemicals,” linked to serious health risks.
postText: Firefighters have relied on firefighting foams for decades, but many contain PFAS, or “forever chemicals,” linked to serious health risks. Meanwhile, Congress just approved an extension to the deadline to eliminate them. A new alternative is gaining traction 👇

claimText: Hundreds of military bases have #PFAS contamination.
postText: Hundreds of military bases have #PFAS contamination. Yet Congress is now going to let the #Pentagon keep using #foreverchemicals in firefighting foam, reports @RachelFrazin? This makes no sense. @EWG #MAHA @pirg

claimText: PFAS, also known as “forever chemicals,” are associated with #cancer and can be found in many products, such as #firefighter foam.
postText: 🧯PFAS, also known as “forever chemicals,” are associated with #cancer and can be found in many products, such as #firefighter foam. 📖Data from @HAZMAT_journal showed firefighters can have a large amount of PFAS on their gear, including breathing masks.

claimText: PFAS persist in the human body, disrupt hormones, weaken immunity, damage the liver, and increase cancer risk.
postText:
PubMedia
PFAS, often called “forever chemicals,” are not an abstract environmental issue anymore. They are a real, proven public-health threat. In Europe, PFAS production was stopped after drinking water contamination, long-term health damage, and court convictions—most notably in Italy. Scientific
